sources.title: P.G. Sorenson Lecture: Dr. Steven Woods, CEO of Kinitos, Inc.
sources.person_relevance: Official University of Saskatchewan lecture page documenting Steven Woods as CEO of Kinitos in 2003, useful for Steven's public-appearance timeline.
sources.notes: Official university page says Woods left AOL in May 2002 to co-found Kinitos and was serving as CEO by the 2003 lecture.

sources.title: Microsoft Offers Sneak Peek to Developers
sources.person_relevance: Useful for Kinitos product context, but not a strong standalone Steven-centric source for the top-level hub.
sources.notes: InternetNews coverage of DevDay 2004 names Kinitos' Smart Client Application Management software as a Microsoft-aligned example.

sources.title: Microsoft Experience Capital Markets Is Supported by More Than 20 Leading Solution Partners
sources.person_relevance: Official Microsoft source confirming Kinitos' product positioning in the enterprise application-management phase of Steven Woods' post-AOL company-building work.
sources.notes: Microsoft lists Kinitos as a solution partner and describes its on-demand application delivery, update control, usage insight, and error reaction capabilities.

sources.title: Bushnell joins NeoEdge board
sources.person_relevance: Good NeoEdge-era milestone, but mainly company-specific rather than Steven-centric.
sources.notes: GameSpot reports NeoEdge was founded in 2002, had 40 employees, had Alex Terry as CEO, and was operating MostFun.com in beta.

sources.title: NeoEdge Networks launches NeoMOM to measure product tastes
sources.person_relevance: Helpful for NeoEdge product and audience history, but not yet a top-level Steven-hub item.
sources.notes: GamesBeat says NeoEdge's NeoMOM initiative drew on a network of more than 50 publishers, more than 500 games, and over 20 million players.

sources.title: NeoEdge raises $4M for in-game video ads from game-focused VC firm
sources.person_relevance: Important company-financing milestone, though still more company-specific than Steven-centric.
sources.notes: GamesBeat reports a $4M round, 470 games across more than 50 publishers, and distribution on outlets such as Big Fish Games and Yahoo Games.

sources.title: Game ad firm NeoEdge merges with game developer, hires new CEO
sources.person_relevance: Useful later-stage company-history source, but not yet a Steven-hub candidate.
sources.notes: GamesBeat covers the Offspring merger, Lesley Mansford becoming CEO, and NeoEdge's continued ad-tech focus alongside new game development.

sources.title: NeoEdge raises $3M for ad platform for games
sources.person_relevance: Additional business-history source for NeoEdge's later funding and partner footprint; not yet for Steven-centric surfacing.
sources.notes: GamesBeat reports MMV Financial funding, more than 100 million monthly video ad impressions, and partnerships with Yahoo Games and iWin.

sources.title: Double Fusion Expands Its In-Game Advertising With NeoEdge Acquisition
sources.person_relevance: Important closing-era company milestone that may matter to the top-level Steven career timeline in summary form.
sources.notes: Game Developer reports Double Fusion's acquisition of NeoEdge and frames it as an expansion into casual and social in-game advertising.

sources.title: MostFun.com shakes up try-before-you-buy
sources.person_relevance: Helpful for the MostFun experiment inside NeoEdge, but too company-specific for direct Steven-hub surfacing.
sources.notes: Gamezebo describes MostFun as offering around 130 premium casual titles free with a small number of TV-quality video ads.

sources.title: Yahoo! Announces Ad-Supported Online Gaming
sources.person_relevance: Strong official evidence for the NeoEdge/Yahoo Games partnership era, though still more company-specific than Steven-centric.
sources.notes: Yahoo's announcement includes a substantial company description for NeoEdge, naming its NeoARM and NeoAds technology and scale within the Yahoo Games rollout.

sources.title: BLUE NOODLE INC. trademark filing in USPTO Official Gazette
sources.person_relevance: Useful legal/brand artifact for the Blue Noodle phase, but not a Steven-hub source.
sources.notes: Official USPTO gazette shows BLUE NOODLE INC. filing for downloadable and online game-related entertainment services, supporting the Blue Noodle games/studio phase.

sources.title: Blue Noodle Premiers New Video Ad-Bar, Clickstrip, at Game Developers Conference
sources.person_relevance: Strong evidence of the Blue Noodle rebrand and product direction, but company-specific rather than Steven-centric.
sources.notes: Ad Tech Daily says Blue Noodle was formerly NeoEdge and positions Clickstrip plus Yahoo Games, iWin, and RedAtoms partnerships as part of the 2011 strategy.

sources.title: Exclusive: MMV Financial in trouble
sources.person_relevance: Context for the lender side of Blue Noodle's collapse, but not for top-level Steven surfacing.
sources.notes: Fortune reports MMV Financial had closed U.S. offices and stopped writing new business shortly before the Blue Noodle asset crisis.

sources.title: Investor immorality: The strange case of Blue Noodle
sources.person_relevance: Important end-of-company narrative for the archive, but too company-specific and messy for direct Steven-hub use.
sources.notes: Fortune says Blue Noodle had also been known as NeoEdge Networks and GWN, defaulted on debt, had assets seized by MMV, and had a deal in principle to sell assets to Double Fusion.

sources.title: Major Expansion at In-Gaming Ad Leader Double Fusion
sources.company_context: Double Fusion acquisition of NeoEdge assets
sources.person_relevance: Useful closing-stage company source and possible career-timeline context, but not yet a headline Steven-hub item beyond summary use.
sources.notes: Ad Tech Daily explicitly frames the deal as an acquisition of NeoEdge's assets and says those assets added 100M+ monthly impressions plus Canadian distribution.

sources.title: MostFun Games homepage (archived March 20, 2007)
sources.person_relevance: Useful deep-archive evidence for the MostFun experiment and its NeoEdge integration, but too company-specific for direct Steven-hub surfacing.
sources.notes: Archived MostFun homepage promotes the MostFun Game Player, routes downloads through ub.neoedge.com, and includes a visible 'Powered by NeoEdge' footer badge.

sources.title: NeoEdge homepage (archived February 5, 2007)
sources.person_relevance: Strong official site evidence for NeoEdge's own product positioning, but better kept in the deep archive than the Steven-centric hub.
sources.notes: Archived NeoEdge homepage describes patent-pending delivery and advertising technology plus unique dynamic ad insertion and multiple ad placement modes.

sources.title: MostFun.com Game Network - Free Unlimited Premium Games (archived April 7, 2008)
sources.person_relevance: Good direct evidence of MostFun's mature product framing and NeoEdge integration, but still company-specific.
sources.notes: Archived MostFun homepage advertises 100+ free premium games, uses DetailsNeoARM.aspx links, and carries a NeoEdge Networks copyright notice.

sources.title: NeoEdge homepage (archived March 15, 2008)
sources.person_relevance: Useful archive evidence for NeoEdge's three-sided market framing, but not a top-level Steven-hub source.
sources.notes: Archived NeoEdge homepage frames the business across advertisers, partner websites, and game publishers, and links Game of the Week directly into a MostFun NeoARM detail page.

sources.title: BlueNoodle homepage (archived February 8, 2011)
sources.person_relevance: Strong official site artifact for the Blue Noodle phase, but mainly for company-history interpretation rather than the Steven hub.
sources.notes: Archived Blue Noodle homepage calls the company the leading ad platform for casual games, describes casual and social game advertising, and highlights developer monetization plus the MMV financing headline.

sources.title: US20080207328A1 - Interstitial advertising in a gaming environment
sources.company_context: NeoEdge Networks / Blue Noodle / Double Fusion IP chain
sources.person_relevance: Important legal artifact because it names Steven Woods as an inventor and shows a concrete IP-transfer chain from NeoEdge into Double Fusion, but it is still better handled as archive detail than top-level hub copy.

date.display: Patent filed February 23, 2007; assignment chain updated through November 2, 2011

sources.notes: Google Patents shows a NeoEdge patent naming Steven Woods and Kelly Slough as inventors, with legal events tracing assignment to NeoEdge, later lender interests, Blue Noodle name-change handling, and eventual assignment into Double Fusion in 2011.

sources.title: US20100175058A1 - System for providing distraction-free content in a flash-based gaming environment
sources.company_context: NeoEdge Networks / Blue Noodle / Double Fusion IP chain
sources.person_relevance: Strong legal artifact for the Blue Noodle-era Flash advertising technology and its transfer path into Double Fusion, though mainly useful inside the deep company archive.

date.display: Patent filed August 24, 2009; assignment chain updated through November 2, 2011

sources.notes: Google Patents records the NeoEdge to Blue Noodle to Double Fusion assignment path for a Flash-game advertising patent family, reinforcing that the 2011 transaction involved concrete IP assets as well as network distribution.

sources.title: Math and Computing Research Discovery Days: Founding Innovation Keynote: Steven Woods
sources.person_relevance: Official University of Waterloo page that concisely places Steven Woods as co-founder of Quack.com, Kinitos, and NeoEdge Networks, useful for the Steven hub's career overview.
sources.notes: Official Waterloo event page says Woods co-founded Quack.com, Kinitos, and NeoEdge Networks and built teams across Silicon Valley and the Waterloo-Toronto corridor.

sources.title: Blue Noodle 6/30/2006 Capital Raise
sources.person_relevance: Useful for the investor trail, but this is a later venture database summary rather than a strong primary historical source.
sources.notes: Gaebler/VentureDeal reports a $5.485M 2006 round from Jefferson Corner Group and VIMAC Ventures; keep as supporting evidence until corroborated by contemporaneous sources.

sources.title: Blue Noodle 9/29/2009 Capital Raise
sources.person_relevance: Useful investor-trail evidence, but later-database quality keeps it out of the top-level hub.
sources.notes: Gaebler/VentureDeal reports a $4M venture round and says the proceeds were for advertising-services growth and acquisition of premium casual game titles.

sources.title: NeoEdge Networks - Crunchbase Company Profile & Funding
sources.person_relevance: Useful current funding summary for archive coordination, but not a primary historical source and not suitable for direct Steven-hub use.
sources.notes: Crunchbase usefully names four rounds and the investor set, but it is a present-day aggregation rather than contemporaneous reporting.

sources.title: Yahoo to offer ad-supported online games
sources.person_relevance: Potential Steven Woods relevance during the NeoEdge / Yahoo Games partnership era; keep out of the top-level hub until role and context are confirmed.
sources.notes: Direct CNET page not yet captured locally; Techmeme snapshot confirms the title and date cluster.

sources.title: Yahoo! Bolsters Ad-Supported Games
sources.person_relevance: Useful NeoEdge-era company coverage, but not yet strong enough for top-level Steven-centric surfacing.
sources.notes: Tracked as part of the Yahoo Games / NeoEdge discovery cluster; local capture still pending.

sources.title: Yahoo to offer free ad-supported games
sources.person_relevance: Potentially useful for Steven-linked NeoEdge context, but not yet ready for the top-level hub.
sources.notes: Tracked as related coverage of the Yahoo Games rollout using NeoEdge-delivered ads.

sources.title: Yahoo, Sony extend ad-funded games offerings
sources.person_relevance: Follow-on NeoEdge-era coverage with possible Steven relevance, but still in review.
sources.notes: Tracked as follow-on trade coverage connected to the same NeoEdge / Yahoo Games cluster.

sources.title: Yahoo Games shifts to free games in alliance with two game ad start-ups
sources.person_relevance: Secondary aggregation only; useful for tracing contemporaneous coverage but not appropriate for top-level surfacing.
sources.notes: Useful as a verification bridge for the July 10, 2008 coverage cluster and the blocked CNET page.
